title: ChaCha20-Poly1305 and Algorithm Choice · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible portable AEAD selection and compatibility matrix with one primitive, parameter, nonce, context, key, parser, trust, timing, migration, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in portable AEAD selection and compatibility matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Use ChaCha20-Poly1305 through its AEAD contract with correct key, nonce, tag, additional-data, and failure handling.
Distinguish standard and XChaCha nonce sizes, interoperability, random-nonce design, and protocol compatibility.
Choose AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305 from platform acceleration, protocol requirements, misuse risk, compliance, interoperability, and measured performance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Chacha Aead Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug cryptographic engineering team handles case 1298 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to select and verify standard or XChaCha20-Poly1305 with exact sizes, AAD, vectors, and interoperability; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use ChaCha20-Poly1305 through its AEAD contract with correct key, nonce, tag, additional-data, and failure handling.

Inspect the Crypto Go Chacha Aead Contract trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Aead Cross Implementation
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crypto-go-aead-cross-implementation-1ahcjw" that uses case 4253 to select and verify standard or XChaCha20-Poly1305 with exact sizes, AAD, vectors, and interoperability. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import cryptographic packages, generate or handle production keys, open sockets, contact TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, compliance services, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, standard crypto APIs, authoritative vectors, fuzz, race, native side-channel measurement, interoperability, controlled network and key-custody systems, load, fault, recovery, compliance, and production gates.

content.text: A debug cryptographic engineering team handles case 7635 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to select and verify standard or XChaCha20-Poly1305 with exact sizes, AAD, vectors, and interoperability; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
